name: GR 92: Altafulla and Tamarit Coastal Path
name_original: Camí de ronda de Tamarit - Ida y vuelta desde Altafulla
description: Hike the moderate 7.2-mile Camí de ronda de Tamarit - Round trip from Altafulla, exploring beaches, forests, and historic sites.
region_intro: Hiking around Altafulla offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea to historical sites and a river estuary. The region features the GR 92, also known as the Camí de Ronda, providing scenic walks with views of cliffs and coves. Inland, trails pass by historical landmarks such as Tamarit Castle, the Roman Villa of Els Munts, and Altafulla Castle. The Mouth of the Gaià River nature reserve adds a natural highlight with its abundant fauna and riverbank trails. Best hiking trails around Altafulla The most popular hiking route is GR 92: Altafulla and Tamarit Coastal Path, a 7.2 miles (11.65 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic coastal views and passes by Tamarit Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Playa de Altafulla – Torredembarra Lighthouse loop from Torredembarra, an easy 3.1 miles (4.91 km) path. This trail showcases coastal scenery, including Canyadell Beach and the Torredembarra lighthouse. Local hikers also love the View of Tamarit Castle – Tamarit Castle loop from Altafulla, a 5.4 miles (8.64 km) trail leading through coastal areas and offering views of Tamarit Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Altafulla is defined by coastal paths, historical sites, and river estuaries. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Altafulla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Altafulla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Altafulla?
A: Altafulla offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 28 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Altafulla?
A: The terrain around Altafulla is quite diverse. You'll find scenic coastal paths, often part of the GR 92 or Camí de Ronda, offering breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea, cliffs, and hidden coves. Inland, trails pass through natural reserves, along riverbanks, and by historical sites, sometimes involving gentle inclines. The region offers a mix of flat, easy paths and moderate routes with some elevation changes.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Altafulla?
A: The Costa Daurada generally enjoys a mild climate, making hiking pleasant for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with comfortable temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season. Winters are typically mild, allowing for enjoyable walks as well.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Altafulla?
A: The hiking trails in Altafulla are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning coastal views, the blend of natural beauty with historical landmarks, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Altafulla has plenty of easy hiking options. Approximately 18 of the available routes are classified as easy. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31551483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Altafulla – Torredembarra Lighthouse loop from Torredembarra&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.1-mile path showcasing coastal scenery and Canyadell Beach.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Altafulla?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31776300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Tamarit Castle – Tamarit Castle loop from Altafulla&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 5.4-mile trail that offers excellent views of Tamarit Castle.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking in Altafulla?
A: Altafulla's trails are rich in history. You can encounter several significant landmarks, including the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/841251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tamarit Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched dramatically on a cliff overlooking the sea. The UNESCO World Heritage site of the Roman Villa of Els Munts is another notable stop. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/771672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Altafulla&lt;/a&gt; and the historic Vila Closa in Altafulla's old town are often accessible from hiking paths.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts several natural highlights. The Mouth of the Gaià River is a significant wildlife nature reserve, perfect for birdwatching. Coastal paths offer stunning viewpoints of the Mediterranean, including impressive red rock cliffs and the Bramadora Rock near Torredembarra. You can also enjoy views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5701350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Jovera Beach and Tamarit Castle&lt;/a&gt; from various points along the coast.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Altafulla dog-friendly?
A: Many of the outdoor areas and trails around Altafulla are generally dog-friendly, especially the natural paths and coastal walks. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in nature reserves like the Mouth of the Gaià River, to protect local wildlife. Always check local signage for specific restrictions.

Q: What are some of the best coastal walks in the area?
A: The coastal paths are a major draw in Altafulla. The GR 92, also known as the Camí de Ronda, offers spectacular sea views. A recommended moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951852272/gr-92-altafulla-and-tamarit-coastal-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 92: Altafulla and Tamarit Coastal Path&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.2 miles long and passes by Tamarit Castle. Another popular option leads from Altafulla to Torredembarra, showcasing impressive red rock cliffs and Canyadell Beach.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While Altafulla is known for its accessible trails, there is at least one route classified as difficult among the 28 available hikes. These more challenging options typically involve greater distances, more significant elevation changes, or rougher terrain, providing a good test for experienced hikers.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Altafulla?
A: Altafulla is well-connected within the Costa Daurada region. While specific public transport details for every trailhead aren't provided, the village itself is accessible by train and bus, making it possible to reach starting points for many coastal and inland walks. It's recommended to check local bus and train schedules for routes connecting to specific areas of interest.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Altafulla?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Altafulla, especially near popular beaches and the village center. For specific trailheads, you may find designated parking areas or street parking in nearby residential zones. It's always a good idea to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Camí de ronda de Tamarit - Round trip from Altafulla?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Altafulla. While specific parking details aren't provided, Altafulla is accessible by public transport, and parking options are generally available in coastal towns. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse outdoor experience, but specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Bosc de la Marquesa, and to be mindful of other hikers and wildlife. Some beaches along the route may have restrictions during peak season.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The cooler seasons are ideal for hiking this route. It's highly recommended to avoid the summer months due to high temperatures and larger crowds, which can detract from the experience.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike, especially for the Tamarit-Punta de la Móra area?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for hiking this trail or accessing the natural areas like the Bosc de la Marquesa within the Tamarit-Punta de la Móra region. These areas are generally open for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The full round trip, which is approximately 11.7 km (7.3 miles) with about 100-110 meters (330-360 feet) of elevation change, can take over 3 hours to complete. Shorter, easier sections are also available.

faqs.question: Is the Camí de ronda de Tamarit suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While the full route is considered moderate and requires good fitness, there are easier sections. A shorter 6 km version with minimal elevation gain (25-30 meters) is suitable for children aged 5 and up who are accustomed to walking. Hikers should be sure-footed, especially on rocky parts.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain, including unpaved and natural paths, gravel, paved sections, and asphalt. You will traverse coves, navigate cliffs, and pass through Mediterranean forest. Be prepared for some rocky and potentially slippery sections, and parts of the route involve walking directly on the beach.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers excellent coastal scenery and historical sites. You'll pass by the historic Tamarit Castle, the circular La Mora Tower, and the protected Bosc de la Marquesa. Stunning coves like Cala Fonda (Waikiki Beach) and Cala Roca Plana, where a Roman pier was discovered, are also highlights. Other notable spots include Jovera Cove and the Port of Altafulla.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Catalunya E25, El Camí IT6TR6 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, and the Camí de Ronda de Tamarit.

faqs.question: Are there facilities or water sources available along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through coastal areas and near settlements like Altafulla and Tamarit, where you can find cafes, pubs, and restaurants. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient water, especially for longer sections, as dedicated water sources directly on the trail might be limited.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, appropriate footwear with good grip is highly recommended, especially for rocky and potentially slippery sections. Bring enough water, sun protection, and snacks. If hiking in cooler months, layers are advisable. Don't forget your camera for the scenic coastal views!
